### Post by zubat on 2008-02-10
I have been trying to get tv out working on my fx5500 nvidia card but I  never get any output onto the tv, I have tried editing xorg.conf in various ways but no luck my current config is:
```

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Thu Dec 13 19:10:32 PST 2007

# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"

	# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
	#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
    Identifier     "layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    Screen      1  "Screen1" RightOf "Screen0"
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

    Load           "glx"

    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY

### Post by arkara on 2008-02-10
ok
you can try nvidia-settings.
this will find your second screen and you will be able to configure it.
i suggest to use the option to write the changes on a new x file and then replace it to xorg.xonf
be sure that you have backed up this one dude
and also make sure that you have used the closed source drivers..
this will probably make the screen work

---

### Post by overdrank on 2008-02-10
> **zubat said:**
> Unfortunatley  still no dice, but this might help when I click detect displays it says TV-0 Is unpluggd which its not.

HI and did you reconfigure you xorg as suggested by arkara? You can try the command 
```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
``` then try the nvidia-settings again. I believe the changes you have made to the xorg are complicating things.
